name: Salt-Rising Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 medium-sized potato, peeled and sliced thin
recipeInstructions:
1. Add the sugar, cornmeal, and salt to the boiling water. Keep the mixture warm overnight at 95-100°F (35-38°C).
2. The next morning, drain out the potato (discard it) and add the scalded sweet milk.
3. Stir in flour until stiff, like a muffin mix. Keep warm at 100-110°F (38-43°C) until it rises.
4. Add the warm water, sugar, salt, lard, and additional flour to form bread dough. Do not knead.
5. Place the dough into bread pans and let rise until doubled in bulk.
6. Bake in a moderate oven at 375°F (190°C) for 45 minutes to an hour. Ensure the dough never chills.
